checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=168372672.47 (2012-06-30) growing to AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=236017924.29 (2013-06-30), a 40.2% increase, while Revenues=1392268463.60 (FY2012) grew to Revenues=1752330631.05 (FY2013), only 25.9%
evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 balance sheet / income statement, comparative FY2012 10-K 0000910406-13-000023

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=313535600.80 (2012-06-30) rose to IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=503303745.96 (2013-06-30), a 60.5% increase, versus Revenues growth of 25.9% (Revenues=1392268463.60 FY2012 to Revenues=1752330631.05 FY2013)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: Gross margin declined from GrossProfit=572963283.56/Revenues=2175520554.86 = 26.3% (FY2014) to GrossProfit=624910440.69/Revenues=2715866349.37 = 23.0% (FY2015), even as revenue grew 24.8%

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Revenues grew at a tightly clustered rate every fiscal year: Revenues=1119823686.36 (FY2011, +24.6%), Revenues=1392268463.60 (FY2012, +24.3%), Revenues=1752330631.05 (FY2013, +25.9%), Revenues=2175520554.86 (FY2014, +24.2%), Revenues=2715866349.37 (FY2015, +24.8%), while quarterly OCF was materially more volatile (e.g., NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=2640593.28 for Q1 FY2015 vs 54153375.84 for Q1 FY2014)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2015 selected financial data / cash flow statement

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2802221.02 (2013-12-31) fell to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=905115.37 (2015-06-30) even as AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 254953624.75 (2013-12-31) to 323454493.45 (2015-06-30); separately AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=85769783.04 (2014-06-30) declined to AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=79972397.88 (2015-06-30) while Revenues grew from 2175520554.86 (FY2014) to 2715866349.37 (FY2015)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2015 balance sheet and receivables/accrued liabilities notes

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?
Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Financial series data spans from FY2009 (period start 2008-07-01) through FY2015 (period end 2015-06-30) with both annual and quarterly granularity across Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, cash flow, balance sheet, and reserve line items

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reduction/release of bad debt reserve (lower bad debt expense) relative to a growing receivables base, which would understate the allowance, overstate net realizable receivables, and flow through to overstate net income

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2802221.02 (2013-12-31) declined to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=905115.37 (2015-06-30) while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 254953624.75 (2013-12-31) to 323454493.45 (2015-06-30), pushing the allowance ratio from about 1.1% to 0.28% of receivables

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Under-accrual of operating expenses/liabilities as revenue scaled, effectively deferring expense recognition and understating current liabilities, thereby overstating operating and net income

rationale_evidence.quote: AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=85769783.04 (2014-06-30) declined to AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=79972397.88 (2015-06-30) even as Revenues grew from 2175520554.86 (FY2014) to 2715866349.37 (FY2015), a 24.8% revenue increase against a 6.8% decline in accrued liabilities

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potentially aggressive purchase price allocation in acquisitions that overweights long-lived intangible/goodwill values relative to revenue growth generated, understating amortization expense and overstating asset values and earnings during high-growth M&A periods

rationale_evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=313535600.80 (2012-06-30) increased to IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=503303745.96 (2013-06-30), a 60.5% jump versus 25.9% revenue growth (Revenues=1392268463.60 FY2012 to Revenues=1752330631.05 FY2013)

top_signals: Allowance for doubtful accounts fell from ~1.1% to ~0.28% of receivables between 2013-12-31 and 2015-06-30 despite growing AR balances
Accrued liabilities declined in absolute terms in FY2015 while revenue grew nearly 25%, reducing accrued liabilities as a % of revenue from ~3.9% to ~2.9%
Intangible assets (excl. goodwill) grew 49.7%-60.5% in FY2012-FY2013, far outpacing 24-26% revenue growth, consistent with aggressive purchase accounting during an acquisitive period
Revenue growth was unusually consistent (24-26%) for five consecutive fiscal years despite far more volatile quarterly operating cash flow
Gross margin declined from ~28% to ~23% over the period, with a notable drop in FY2015
